articles.title: Meanwood Community Nursery and Primary School to Reopen on 3 September 2026
articles.excerpt: Meanwood Community Nursery and Primary School will reopen for the new academic year on Thursday, 3 September 2026, according to the latest announcement.
articles.content_html:
Meanwood Community Nursery and Primary School will reopen on Thursday, 3 September 2026. This date marks the beginning of the new academic year for students at the school.

The announcement was made on the school-term-dates.co.uk website, providing clarity for parents and guardians regarding the school calendar.

Families are advised to prepare for the return of students on this date, ensuring that all necessary arrangements are in place for the start of term.

For further details regarding the school year, parents can refer to the school's official communications or the school-term-dates.co.uk website.

articles.title: Hollingworth Academy in Rochdale Shares Updates on Instagram
articles.excerpt: Hollingworth Academy in Rochdale has been actively sharing updates on its Instagram account, engaging with the community and showcasing school activities.
articles.content_html:
Hollingworth Academy in Rochdale has been using its Instagram account to share updates and engage with the local community. The account, @hollingworth_ac, features posts about various school activities and events.

The academy aims to keep parents and students informed about important announcements and achievements through social media. This initiative is part of a broader effort to enhance communication with families.

By showcasing student work and school events, Hollingworth Academy fosters a sense of community and pride among its members. The posts also provide a platform for celebrating student successes.

For more information and to view the latest updates, visit Hollingworth Academy's Instagram page.

articles.title: Rochdale Students Secure Second Place in National Film Competition
articles.excerpt: Students from Skills 4 All in Rochdale have achieved second place in a national film competition with a film on online safety and artificial intelligence.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 15 July 2026 at 08:44. 2 source updates have been combined into this article.

Students from Skills 4 All in Rochdale have achieved second place in a national film competition with a film on online safety and artificial intelligence.

Latest update

A group of students from Skills 4 All in Rochdale has won second place in a national film competition. The students created a film that addresses the responsible use of artificial intelligence and promotes online safety.

The competition showcased entries from various educational institutions across the country, highlighting the importance of digital literacy among young people.

The film produced by the Rochdale students was praised for its creativity and relevance, reflecting current issues surrounding technology and safety online.

This achievement not only recognises the hard work of the students but also underscores the commitment of Skills 4 All to equip learners with essential skills for the digital age.

Earlier developments

The competition aimed to raise awareness about the importance of online safety in the digital age. Participants were encouraged to explore various themes related to technology and its impact on society.

The film produced by the Rochdale students was praised for its creativity and relevance, highlighting the need for responsible behaviour online. Their achievement reflects the growing emphasis on digital literacy in education.

This recognition not only showcases the talents of the students but also underscores the commitment of Skills 4 All to equip young people with essential skills for the future.

Update timeline

  • 15 July 2026, 08:44: Rochdale Students Secure Second Place in National Film Competition

articles.title: Potting Shed Returns to The Rock for Summer Holiday Activities
articles.excerpt: The Potting Shed is back at The Rock, offering a range of family activities throughout the summer holidays.
articles.content_html:
A popular family attraction, the Potting Shed, has returned to The Rock in Rochdale for the summer holidays. The venue will host a variety of activities designed for children and families to enjoy together.

Activities include arts and crafts, gardening workshops, and interactive games, providing an engaging environment for families during the school break.

The Potting Shed aims to create a fun and educational experience, encouraging children to explore nature and develop their creativity.

Families are encouraged to visit The Rock to take part in these activities, which will run throughout the summer holiday period.

articles.title: Health and Safety Day Event Held in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Occupational Health and Didac hosted a Health and Safety Day event, focusing on workplace safety and health awareness.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Occupational Health and Didac organised a Health and Safety Day event on July 14, 2026. The event aimed to raise awareness about health and safety practices in the workplace.

Participants engaged in discussions and activities designed to enhance understanding of occupational health risks and safety measures.

The initiative reflects a commitment to improving workplace conditions and ensuring the well-being of employees in the Rochdale area.

articles.title: Middleton Big Dig uncovers evidence of medieval Tonge Hall
articles.excerpt: More than 350 people, including pupils from five local schools, took part in the Middleton Big Dig and uncovered evidence of activity dating from the medieval period.
articles.summary: The community archaeological dig uncovered medieval ridge-and-furrow features and structures believed to relate to an earlier hall that stood on the Tonge Hall site before the existing Tudor building.
articles.content_html:
Evidence of medieval farming and structures connected with an earlier version of Tonge Hall has been uncovered during a community archaeological dig in Middleton.

More than 350 people took part in the Middleton Big Dig, including pupils from five local schools.

Rochdale Borough Council said participants found evidence of activity spanning from the medieval period to the present day.

Medieval landscape discovered

Among the discoveries was evidence of ridge-and-furrow farming, a landscape feature created by historic ploughing methods widely used during the Middle Ages.

Archaeologists and volunteers also uncovered structures believed to relate to the original medieval hall that existed before the Tudor building now standing on the site.

The surviving building is known as Tonge Hall.

More than 350 people involved

The project involved residents, volunteers and children from five schools, giving young people the opportunity to take part in practical archaeology and learn more about Middleton’s history.

Pupils from St Peter’s RC Primary School were among those who visited the site and helped examine finds uncovered during the dig.

Photographs shared by the council show pupils working alongside members of the archaeological team and examining material recovered from the excavation area.

Finds to be cleaned and catalogued

The objects and other material discovered during the project will now be cleaned, recorded and catalogued.

Once that work has been completed, the finds are expected to be offered to Touchstones Rochdale for inclusion in its collection.

The dig formed part of the activity programme supporting the restoration of the Broadfield Park Slopes.

The wider project has received funding from the National Lottery Heritage Fund.

This report is based on a public update and photographs published by Rochdale Borough Council following the completion of the Middleton Big Dig.
articles.event_location: Tonge Hall and Broadfield Park Slopes, Middleton
articles.right_to_reply: Rochdale Borough Council, the Middleton Big Dig organisers, participating schools and anyone materially affected by this report may submit a response or request a factual correction by emailing news@rochdaledaily.co.uk.

articles.title: UK Management College Celebrates 10-Year Milestone at SummerFest 2026
articles.excerpt: UK Management College marked its 10-year anniversary with SummerFest 2026 at Heaton Park, attended by local dignitaries and community members.
articles.content_html:
A celebration took place at Heaton Park, Manchester, as UK Management College (UKMC) hosted its SummerFest 2026 to mark a decade of service and community impact. The event featured various activities and was attended by local dignitaries, including the Lord Mayors of Manchester, Tameside, and Rochdale.

The SummerFest aimed to highlight the college's contributions to the local community over the past ten years. Attendees enjoyed a range of performances, food stalls, and interactive sessions designed to engage the public and showcase the college's achievements.

UKMC has played a significant role in the educational landscape of the region, providing opportunities for students and fostering partnerships with local businesses and organisations.

As the college looks ahead, it aims to continue its commitment to education and community engagement, ensuring that it remains a vital part of the Rochdale area.

articles.title: Concerns Raised Over Effectiveness of Rochdale's Exclusion Zone
articles.excerpt: Residents express worries about the functioning of the exclusion zone in Rochdale's Chernobyl area, questioning its effectiveness in ensuring safety.
articles.content_html:
Residents in Rochdale have raised concerns about the effectiveness of the exclusion zone in the Chernobyl area. Many fear that the measures in place are not adequately protecting the community from potential hazards.

The exclusion zone was established to mitigate risks associated with the area's history, but some locals believe it is not functioning as intended. They have called for a review of the current safety protocols.

Local authorities have been urged to assess the situation and ensure that the exclusion zone is serving its purpose effectively.
